top_support_or_feedback_clusters.summary: Next 12 Biggest Wins is the active pressure cluster: finish install-specific trust/support truth, creator publication and shelf posture, pulse-v3 launch governance, and no-step-back utility parity.
Downloads, updates, support closure, and channel-aware trust copy now exist as first-party surfaces and must keep moving in lockstep instead of drifting back into separate promises.
The current longest pole is Core Engine, so release, support, and publication decisions should assume that this lane still sets the pacing risk for the broader public product.

governor_decisions.reason: Keep delivery focus on Next 12 Biggest Wins. The pulse shows 99% overall progress in 'Public-fit polish', history depth has reached 22 snapshots, and the pacing risk remains concentrated in Core Engine while trust/publication/utility closure still needs measured completion on the active registry.
Freeze launch expansion until published flagship readiness proof returns to ready posture.
